rulings.Case Title: LINDSAY DUFFY-SMITH VS. ALKA AGRAWAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 9, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT ALKA AGRAWAL, RAVIN AGRAWAL Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And To Impose Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MORDECHAI D. PELTA et al VS. BRUCE M. MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 9, 2012, Line 15. PLAINTIFFS MORDECHAI PELTA AND SARAH PELTA'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENA. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Cohen as follows: Motion to quash subpoena of Dr. Pelta's records is granted. Dr. Pelta was a pre-litigation consultant, whose materials are entitled to qualified work product protection absent good cause justifying discovery. See Weil & Brown, California Civil Procedure Before trial section 8:246 et seq. Defendants are not prejudiced by their inability to review these documents. If Dr. Pelta is later designated as an expert or to otherwise testify, his materials will be discoverable. Motion to quash subpoena of employment records granted in part and denied in part. Defendants shall be entitled to inspect documents related to wages and other employment earnings, they shall also be entitled to inspect documents relating or referring to Mr. Pelta's physical condition and medical restrictions, if any. The court adds the following: Defendant to issue a new subpoena that comports with this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: THANH "LEANNE" VU VS. FIRST REPUBLIC BANK, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Arb And Stay Proceedings; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 9, 2012, Line 13. DEFENDANT FIRST REPUBLIC BANK, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, ANNA HIRANO AND AFSANEH IRANPOUR'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. The motion is granted in its entirety. Although there is a high degree of procedural unconscionability, there is no substantive unconscionability and the Arbitration Agreement is therefore enforceable. The individual Defendants are entitled to claim the benefits of the Arbitration Agreement. Although Plaintiff's claims against the individual Defendants are not based exclusively on any agency relationship between Defendant First Republic and the individual Defendants, the claims are based in part on such agency. Moreover, the claims against the individual Defendants are inextricably intertwined with Plaintiff's claims against Defendant First Republic and the Agreement, broadly construed, covers all claims arising out of Plaintiff's employment with Defendant First Republic. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CARIDEO VS. STANLEY KELLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Francene Kelley Kennedy'S Application For Order Granting 1- Leave To Intervene; 2- Leave To File Verified Joinder To And/Or Intervention; 3- Confirming Consolidation Of Related Action For Trial Purposes
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 9, 2012, Line 16. Francene Kelley Kennedy'S Application For Order Granting 1- Leave To Intervene; 2- Leave To File Verified Joinder To And/Or Intervention; 3- Confirming Consolidation Of Related Action For Trial Purposes. The motion is granted in part and denied in part. The request to permit intervention is granted pursuant to Cal. Civ. Pro. § 387(b). The request to confirm consolidation of the civil action with the probate proceedings for trial is denied without prejudice to its be noticed and heard in the Probate Department. . The current trial date of June 18, 2012 in the probate proceedings is continued to December 3, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JJM)
